clusters.title: Semiconductor Industry Trends
clusters.summary: The semiconductor industry continues to evolve with advancements like Intel's 2nm 3.5D AI processor and Samsung's Exynos chips in future Galaxy devices.

clusters.title: Intel's 2nm 3.5D AI Processor
clusters.summary: Intel has begun shipping the industry's first 2nm custom compute SoC built on its 3.5D eXtreme Dimension System in Package (XDSiP) platform to Japan's Fujitsu, marking a concrete step from roadmap promise to commercial deployment in the AI infrastructure.

clusters.title: Samsung's Exynos Chips
clusters.summary: Samsung's Exynos chips haven't yet built a good reputation, and the company has flip-flopped between Exynos and Snapdragon processors in its Galaxy S lineup over the past few years. The recently announced Galaxy S26 and Galaxy S26+ use the Exynos 2600, while the upcoming Galaxy S27 will likely switch back to the Exynos 2900.

stocks.ai_explanation: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co., Ltd. (TSMC) is a leading global semiconductor manufacturer providing advanced semiconductor solutions and services. This is an educational summary, not financial advice.
Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) is a leading supplier of microprocessors and graphics processing units. This is an educational summary, not financial advice.

Applied Materials, Inc. (ASML) is a Dutch multinational equipment company specializing in photolithography systems used in semiconductor manufacturing. This is an educational summary, not financial advice.

International Business Machines Corporation (IBM) is a multinational technology company that provides information technology, consulting, and related services. This is an educational summary, not financial advice.
Qualcomm Incorporated (QCOM) is a leading provider of wireless communication technologies and components. This is an educational summary, not financial advice.

disclaimer: Educational use only. Not financial advice.
